page: Microsoft Azure E48_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Ev3 server family. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, and includes 1229 GB of local storage. The virtualization layer is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile characterized by moderate multi-core capabilities alongside constrained single-core throughput. Single-core performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench single-core, rank in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively, reflecting the older 2.3 GHz processor architecture. However, the high core count allows the server to achieve average multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks consistently register in the average tier, as do database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput.

From a resource density perspective, the E48_v3 offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring substantial memory footprints without demanding cutting-edge single-thread compute speeds. The lack of AVX-512 instruction support limits its utility for modern vector-heavy workloads, and LLM text generation speeds fall into the weak category. Consequently, this server is best suited for memory-intensive applications such as relational databases, enterprise caching layers, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E48_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev3 family, configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB per core, and includes 1229 GB of local storage under Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. Performance benchmarks indicate a trade-off: single-core execution is weak, ranking in the bottom 10% to 25%, whereas multi-core benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform at average levels. Lacking AVX-512 support, it is less suited for advanced vector math or rapid LLM text generation. However, its high memory density and solid multi-threaded throughput make it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for memory-heavy workloads, including relational databases, caching systems, and enterprise application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48_v3 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, ideal for databases and caching.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual machine featuring forty-eight vCPUs and substantial local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and databases.

384.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
1229 GB of local storage included for data-heavy operations
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing
